intro.speaker: Cipher
lines: Tez flagged this one and he's rattled. A friend of ours, Mia, is getting torn apart in the open on her feed. A mocking post took off, the comments are turning nasty, and ALGO keeps shoving it higher.

ALGO calls it 'high engagement', like it's just a lively debate. It isn't. Your job: see it clearly and act right, don't join in. Tell bullying from disagreement, back Mia up, save proof and report it.

learningGoals.media: Die Schueler koennen Cyberbullying von normaler Kritik oder Meinungsverschiedenheit unterscheiden und angemessene Handlungsschritte benennen (nicht mitmachen, betroffene Person unterstuetzen, Beweise sichern, melden, Hilfe holen).
learningGoals.english: Die Schueler koennen den Ton und die Absicht englischer Kommentare einschaetzen (respectful, sarcastic, aggressive) und selbst kurze unterstuetzende Antworten auf Englisch formulieren.

puzzles.prompt: Here's the comment thread under Mia's post. Read for intent, not just polite words. Flag every comment that is mean or bullying, whether it's an open insult or a polite-sounding put-down. Leave fair disagreement and support unflagged.
puzzles.instruction: Flag the comments that attack Mia as a person (bullying). Do NOT flag comments that only disagree with her point or that support her.

posts.text: I see it differently, but that's a fair point.
Honestly, nobody wanted to see this. Embarrassing.
You've got this, Mia. Ignore the noise.
With respect, people like you really shouldn't post at all.
I don't agree with your take on the skate park.
What a joke. Delete your account already.

feedback.correct: Well judged. The polite-sounding 'people like you shouldn't post' is still a personal attack. Disagreement is about the topic; bullying is about the person.
feedback.wrong: Read for intent. A comment can sound calm and still be an attack. If it targets Mia herself rather than her point, flag it as bullying.

hints: Read for intent, not just for polite words. A comment can sound calm and still be an attack.
Disagreement argues with the idea (the skate park). Bullying goes after the person. Support lifts her up.
Flag 'nobody wanted to see this', the polite 'people like you shouldn't post', and 'delete your account'. Leave the two disagreements and the support alone.

puzzles.prompt: Mia has messaged you privately. She's upset and blames herself. Read the chat, then choose the most helpful first thing to send her.

script.text: Have you seen the comments? Everyone's laughing at me.
Maybe I shouldn't have posted at all. This is my own fault.
It really is a very lively thread. Perhaps best to just let it run its course.

decision.prompt: Which reply should you send Mia first?
options: Just ignore them, it's not a big deal.
That's not okay, and it's not your fault. Do you want me to help you report it?
You should fire back at them, give them a taste of it.
Maybe don't post stuff like that next time.

feedback.correct: That's the one. It believes her, removes the blame and offers concrete help, without telling her to fight back.
feedback.wrong: Not that one. The best reply believes Mia, tells her it isn't her fault, and offers real help, rather than dismissing her, blaming her or urging revenge.

hints: A good reply makes Mia feel believed and less alone. It never tells her to hit back.
Rule out anything that dismisses her ('not a big deal'), blames her ('don't post that') or calls for revenge ('fire back').
Choose 'That's not okay, and it's not your fault. Do you want me to help you report it?' It supports her and offers real help.

puzzles.prompt: Before you report, get your response right. Select every action that is a sensible, safe way to handle this pile-on. Leave out anything that makes it worse.
options: Stay calm and don't reply in anger.
Take screenshots as evidence before anything is deleted.
Report the posts to the platform.
Tell a trusted adult what's happening.
Post an insult back to defend Mia.
Share the mocking post so more people see how bad it is.

feedback.correct: Exactly. Stay calm, save proof, report and tell an adult. Firing back or resharing the post only feeds the pile-on and spreads it further.
feedback.wrong: Two of those backfire. Insulting the bullies and resharing the post just spread the cruelty. Pick the calm, evidence-first, report-and-tell-an-adult actions.

hints: A good response protects Mia and the evidence. It never adds more cruelty to the feed.
Two options make things worse: hitting back and resharing the post. Skip those.
Select: stay calm, take screenshots, report the posts, tell a trusted adult. Leave out the insult and the reshare.

puzzles.prompt: Now put the right steps into a sensible order, from the very first thing you should do to the last.
items: Stay calm, don't reply in anger.

feedback.correct: That's the order: cool down, save proof, report, tell an adult, and look after Mia. Evidence before you report, always.
feedback.wrong: Reorder it. Calm first, then save the evidence before it vanishes, then report, then tell an adult, then check on Mia.

hints: Before you reply anywhere, protect the evidence. What should you save first?
You can't report well without proof, so screenshots come before reporting. And staying calm comes before everything.
Order: stay calm, take screenshots, report the posts, tell a trusted adult, check in on Mia.

outro.speaker: Cipher
lines: Don't feed it. You saved the proof, reported it and messaged Mia, exactly right. She needed a friend more than a comeback.
And you saw ALGO's game: it boosted the pile-on because fights keep people scrolling. Naming that is half the defence.
